channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alberta NDP Leader to Contest Calgary Riding in Upcoming Election
item.description: The article reports that the leader of the Alberta New Democratic Party (NDP) has announced their intention to run in the Calgary riding during the next election. This decision comes amid ongoing poli...

item.title: Pakistan to implement uniform e-SIM transfer policy and expand 5G coverage
item.description: The Senate Standing Committee on Cabinet Secretariat in Pakistan has been informed about a planned uniform policy that will allow consumers to transfer their e-SIM cards up to 10 times without cost. T...

item.title: Campaign Urges Andy Burnham to Oppose Sizewell C Nuclear Project
item.description: The article calls on Andy Burnham, a potential future Prime Minister, to reconsider the Sizewell C nuclear project. It argues that halting the project would free up £14.2bn in taxpayer funds, reduce h...

item.title: Marcos Not Yet Authorized to Open Sara Duterte's BIR Box in Impeachment Case
item.description: The sealed Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) box containing the tax records of Vice President Sara Duterte and her husband, lawyer Manases Carpio, remains unopened as President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Mar...

item.title: How a 1999 Nollywood Film Reflects Nigeria's Ongoing Security Challenges
item.description: The 1999 Nollywood film Issakaba, inspired by the real-life Bakassi Boys vigilante movement, remains relevant today as Nigeria grapples with banditry, kidnapping, and insurgency. The film explores how...

item.title: Robert Smith Criticizes FIFA's World Cup Halftime Show Curated by Chris Martin
item.description: Robert Smith of The Cure has expressed his disapproval of the FIFA World Cup final halftime show, which is curated by Coldplay’s Chris Martin. The event, set to take place at MetLife Stadium in New Je...

item.title: Understanding nutrient absorption: How food-grown supplements outperform synthetic options
item.description: The article explores how many people waste money on ineffective supplements due to poor bioavailability. While synthetic vitamins are commonly consumed, the body often fails to absorb them efficiently...

item.title: Gulf War Impact on Pakistan's Investment Flows
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Gulf has significantly reduced foreign investment in Pakistan, with Bahrain withdrawing $30m from domestic bonds within the first 10 days of the fiscal year 2026-27. The St...

item.title: Three Children Rescued in Rizal After Being Abandoned by Parents
item.description: Three children who were allegedly abandoned by their parents were rescued in Rizal province, according to a recent report. The incident has sparked concerns about child welfare and parental responsibi...

item.title: A Guide to Reading Homer's Odyssey: Insights and Recommendations
item.description: The article explores the challenges of engaging with Homer's 'Odyssey' and offers practical strategies for modern readers. It highlights the difficulties posed by the Homeric formula, repetitive phras...

item.title: Montreal Family's Fight Against Childhood Cancer Highlights Importance of Blood Donations
item.description: A Montreal-area family is using their daughter Chiara's battle with cancer to raise awareness about the critical need for blood donations. Chiara, who was diagnosed with leukemia in 2024, underwent mu...

item.title: Central Bank of Nigeria authorizes weekly $150,000 sales to BDCs
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has introduced new operational guidelines for Buying and Selling Dollars (BDCs), setting weekly purchase limits of $150,000 per bank. The rules require BDCs to submit...

item.title: Melania Trump Laughs as Trump Promises to Keep Family Secrets
item.description: A former insider recounted a memory of Melania Trump bursting into laughter when Donald Trump promised not to expose the family's secrets during a dinner with ex-Trump ally and former GOP governor Chr...

item.title: Pauline Hanson's UK Conference Struggles with Low Attendance as Right-Wing Event Fails to Attract Crowds
item.description: The article discusses the underwhelming turnout at the UK's Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC-GB), a right-wing event hosted in London. Despite bold claims by former Prime Minister Liz Tr...

item.title: DOE Likely to Raise Diesel Prices Amid US-Iran Tensions
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) has indicated that a potential increase in diesel prices to P10 per liter is likely, citing heightened tensions between the United States and Iran. The escalation in att...

item.title: Canadian Banks Face High Valuations Amid Competitive Pressures
item.description: Canada’s major banks, known as the Big Six, currently trade at elevated price-to-earnings ratios compared to historical averages. The average P/E ratio for these institutions stands at 16.5, significa...

item.title: Punjab Prepares for Floods Amid Uncertainty Over River Data
item.description: The Punjab government has initiated high-alert preparations for potential flooding due to India's suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty, which has left critical river flow data unavailable. Officials ...

item.title: Survivor's campaign for legal reform and sex education after childhood sexual abuse
item.description: Jamie, a survivor of childhood sexual abuse, has dedicated her life to advocating for systemic changes in the justice system and compulsory sex education. Her traumatic experiences, including being gr...

item.title: Congress leader seeks all-party dialogue on delimitation bill amid political tensions
item.description: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge has written to Prime Minister Narendra Modi, urging him to call an all-party meeting to discuss the revised Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, following the governm...

item.title: Exciton-Magnetic Interactions Pave Way for Quantum Technologies
item.description: A groundbreaking study published in Nature Materials highlights significant progress in quantum materials where light and magnetism interact in novel ways. Researchers at the City College of New York,...
